compiled_state.title: Doc drift: link relations docstrings list 3 of 6; mcp_server header claims subprocess reads; reconcile docstring understates mutating modes
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
Three verified doc/code mismatches that mislead agent consumers (the primary audience).

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD)
1. Read __init__.py:266 / mcp_server.py:360: link relations documented as "blocks | depends_on | relates_to". Run rebar link A B discovered_from -> succeeds; invalid relation error lists all SIX relations.
2. Read mcp_server.py:4-5: claims reads "use the library's subprocess wrappers" — reads are in-process via _reads.py (its own docstring says so).
3. Read __init__.py:404-409: reconcile docstring says only "live mutates Jira" — bootstrap-strict/bootstrap-throttle mutate too (proven live; see bug said-stilt-brow).

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: docstrings match engine behavior; CLAUDE.md's discovered_from workflow is consistent with the library docs.
- Actual: three contradictions above.

## Fix direction
Correct all three strings. For the relations list, source it from the engine's canonical relation set (single source of truth) or add a doc-conformance test comparing the docstring against the engine's accepted relations so it cannot drift again. Fix item 3 together with the said-stilt-brow gate fix.

## Suggested RED test
Doc-conformance test: parse the six relations from the engine (or its error message) and assert the library/MCP docstrings mention all of them.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All six relations documented in library + MCP link docs
- [ ] mcp_server module docstring describes in-process reads accurately
- [ ] reconcile docstrings name every Jira-mutating mode
- [ ] Conformance test pins the relations list

verify_commands.dd_text: docstrings name all six relations + all mutating reconcile modes
